title: Lepidomicrosorium superficiale
taxonomy: Lepidomicrosorium superficiale is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern found in certain parts of the world. Lepidomicrosorium superficiale is typically classified within the subfamily Polypodioideae. The classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationships with other fern species. Lepidomicrosorium superficiale is often grouped within the genus Lepidomicrosorium.

intro: Lepidomicrosorium superficiale, also known as the 'Creeping Maidenhair', is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepidomicrosorium superficiale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
